auction_style: Bryan Boardman is a casual-but-engaged competitor who builds OF-heavy rosters and then tries to flip his outfield surplus for pitching or positional needs. He goes by 'Boardy' in league parlance. He's enthusiastic at the start of negotiations but frequently pulls back: 'i think i am holding tight for right now' after initial interest. He starts at high prices in trade asks ('Cole and Realmuto needs to get back more than Arenado and McMahon') then recalibrates.
price_tendency_detail: Boardman consistently overvalues his own players in trade asks — he demanded Aaron Judge for Clayton Kershaw (sent that offer), claimed Cole + Realmuto needed 'more than Arenado + McMahon,' and asked for Ohtani as a starting point for Hader. He anchors high on his stars but often comes around to deals.
trade_style: Active initiator but slow follow-through. Boardman puts players 'on the block' frequently and invites trade talk, but then backs away when the season is going well. He works with Chathams extensively — multiple confirmed trades across 2015, 2016, 2018, 2020, 2021, 2022 — suggesting he's comfortable with familiar trading partners. He tends to confirm trades with one-word replies ('confirmed', 'approved', 'good').
notable_tendencies: Historically OF-rich at auction — frequently offers outfield surplus and hunts for pitching or catchers
Consistently overvalues his core assets in initial asks then comes down to fair deals
Work at the NBA (confirmed in 2020 threads — 'crazy busy with start of NBA Re-Start') means he goes dark during NBA crunch periods
Voted himself 'team of the week' for getting the Korea Opening Series games — competitive and self-aware
B Squared era trades: dealt Kershaw for Judge (2018) and Moore for Melky (2016), showing he's not afraid of name trades
intel_summary: Boardman is an OF hoarder who will almost always have surplus outfield by midseason. He's willing to trade but tends to anchor high on his best players — get him engaged early and let him come to you on price. His NBA job means he periodically disappears during the season; if he goes quiet on a trade proposal it may be timing, not disinterest. He and Chathams have a comfortable trading relationship built over many years.
